name: Ravi Coltrane
abstract: Ravi Coltrane (born August 6, 1965) is an American jazz saxophonist. Co-owner of the record label RKM Music, he has produced music for pianist Luis Perdomo, guitarist David Gilmore, and trumpeter Ralph Alessi.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: A Tribute to "Trane"
COSMIC MUSIC: The Celestial Songs of Alice Coltrane
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Chick Corea
about_quotes.evidence: I needed to bring it more this way in terms of me writing and putting my own ideas together with a new band. That was the workshop idea for me and Stanley this week, was some young musicians who are incredible to play with—Ravi Coltrane
about_quotes.doc_title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Joanne Brackeen
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve had Greg Osby do some things, Javon Jackson, Ravi Coltrane, Chris Potter was on one album... One I recorded live at the Jazz Standard that was with Negro, my regular drummer and Ira Coleman and Ravi Coltrane.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joanne Brackeen interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 10, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: So we actually had Joshua, Chris, and Ravi for three gigs... That's how that edition came about.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Mancuso
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Yeah. Speaking of that, Ravi Coltrane came to Hamilton once. And he looked so much like his father. And I was sitting next to him and it was a very weird feeling. [INTERVIEWEE] Eerie, huh?
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Mancuso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 10, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: Sands
about_quotes.evidence: playing with Ravi Coltrane you know, and going to see them in New York and knowing that I can also see them in the lunch room

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes
about_quotes.evidence: they even use Coltrane’s son, Ravi Coltrane with Elvin Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster and Bill Hughes interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Schenectady, New York, March 28, 1995
